_comment: LinkedIn ships fully hashed/atomized CSS class names on this view (e.g. 'aa26e2c6', '_4946ed03') with zero semantic meaning — not just churn-prone, but useless for selectors entirely. Title/company are resolved primarily from document.title (see parseFromDocumentTitle in linkedin.ts); these selectors are a last-resort fallback. Prefer aria-label / attribute-based selectors (href*='/company/') over class names, which can change on every deploy.
_jobDescriptionComment: Verified live 2026-07-12. LinkedIn's job description lives under a container whose 'componentkey' attribute is semantic and stable ('JobDetails_AboutTheJob_{jobId}' — only the numeric jobId suffix changes), unlike the fully hashed CSS classes elsewhere on this view. The actual text sits inside a nested '[data-testid="expandable-text-box"]' span — but that data-testid is NOT unique by itself, since LinkedIn reuses it for the 'About the company'/'Commitments' blurbs too. It must stay scoped under the JobDetails_AboutTheJob container. The older '#job-details'/'.jobs-description__content' guesses never matched and are kept only as a last-resort fallback.
